3.q: SiDi19-3 overexpression increased the transcript levels of most Na+/H+ antiporter (NHX), salt overly sensitive (SOS), and calcineurin B-like protein (CBL) genes and improved the salt tolerance of foxtail millet and Arabidopsis.

4.q: Transient silencing of SiGPX1 disrupted cellular ion homeostasis, impaired reactive oxygen species scavenging capacity (indicated by reduced H2O2 clearance) and elevated malondialdehyde accumulation.

6.q: DAB and NBT staining indicated a deeper coloration in the overexpression lines, suggesting higher reactive oxygen species accumulation.

7.q: SiNCED1 overexpression increased endogenous ABA contents to enhance drought stress tolerance and delay seed dormancy.

8.q: ectopic overexpression of SiNCED1 could enhance drought stress resistance by elevating endogenous ABA levels and promoting stomatal closure.

10.q: Yeast cells co-transformed with the SiPLATZ12-AD and SiDi19-3-BD constructs were able to grow... indicating the physical interaction between SiPLATZ12 and SiDi19-3 in yeast cells; SiPLATZ12-His could be pulled down by SiDi19-3-GST; BiFC YFP signal observed.

11.q: Phosphoinositide-specific Phospholipase C (PI-PLC) plays a crucial role in lipid- and Ca2+-dependent signaling pathways... highlighting its critical role in salt-stress signal transduction in foxtail millet.

16.q: Abscisic acid (ABA) and H2O2 played positive roles in the induction of SiNF-YA1 and SiNF-YB8 under stress treatments.

17.q: Abscisic acid (ABA) and H2O2 played positive roles in the induction of SiNF-YA1 and SiNF-YB8 under stress treatments.

18.q: Integrated transcriptomic–metabolomic analysis pinpointed glutathione metabolism as the central regulatory pathway for saline-alkali tolerance... SiGPX1 is a master positive regulator of saline-alkali tolerance in foxtail millet by maintaining redox homeostasis.

20.q: the SiLTP expression levels were higher in SiARDP-OE plants compared than the WT

21.q: Under salt stress, compared to the Em transgenic foxtail millet seedlings, SiDi19-3-overexpression seedlings raised the transcript levels of all SiCBLs, except SiCBL5 and SiCBL6; all SiNHXs, except SiNHX3; SiSOS1; and SiSOS2.

22.q: Under salt stress, compared to the Em transgenic foxtail millet seedlings, SiDi19-3-overexpression seedlings raised the transcript levels of all SiCBLs, except SiCBL5 and SiCBL6; all SiNHXs, except SiNHX3; SiSOS1; and SiSOS2.

23.q: DPY1 acts upstream of STRESS-ACTIVATED PROTEIN KINASE 6 (SAPK6, a subclass I SnRK2) and is required for full SAPK6 activation, thereby allowing regulation of downstream genes to mount a response against drought stress.

24.q: Electrophoretic mobility shift assays and yeast one-hybrids indicated that the transcription factor ABA-responsive DRE-binding protein (SiARDP) could bind to the dehydration-responsive element of SiLTP promoter in vitro and in vivo

25.q: SiARDP binds to DRE cis-elements in the SiASR4 promoter region both in vivo and in vitro. Moreover, SiASR4 transcription is increased in SiARDP-overexpressing plants.

26.q: yeast one-hybrid assays and dual-luciferase reporter assay further verified that the transcription factors SibHLH49 and SiMYBS1 ... can interact with the promoter of the PROSiCAPE14 gene, regulating its expression.

27.q: Yeast one-hybrid assays and dual-luciferase reporter assay further verified that the transcription factors SibHLH49 and SiMYBS1 can interact with the promoter of the PROSiCAPE14 gene, regulating its expression.

28.q: SiARDP could physically bind to the DREs, but SiAREB1 could not. These results revealed that SiREM6 is a target gene of SiARDP and plays a critical role in high salt stress tolerance.

29.q: SiHAK1 facilitates extreme high-affinity K+ uptake activity under low K+ condition and salt stress... transport activity was regulated by external K+ supply and internal K+ content... involved in the mediation of K homeostasis in S. italica.

30.q: transgenic plants showed increased transcription of the stress-related genes, RD29A, RD29B, RAB18 and RD22, and the ABA-related genes, ABI1 and NCED3 under dehydration conditions.

31.q: SiMYB19 overexpression promotes abscisic acid (ABA) accumulation in transgenic rice and upregulates the ABA synthesis gene OsNCED3

32.q: overexpression of SiCYP19-b significantly increased the proline content ... in foxtail millet

33.q: DPY1 can be phosphorylated and activated in response to osmotic stress and is required for more than half of osmotic stress–induced global phosphorylation events, including the phosphorylation of SnRK2s

34.q: overexpression of SiCYP19-b significantly increased the proline content and reduced the accumulation of reactive oxygen species (ROS) in foxtail millet, compared to that in SiCYP19-a.

35.q: eight genes (SiALDH3E1/SiALDH3E2, SiALDH3H1/SiALDH3H2, SiALDH2B1/SiALDH2B2 and SiALDH18B1/SiALDH18B2) are located in four pairs of segmental duplicated genome regions.

36.q: eight genes (SiALDH3E1/SiALDH3E2, SiALDH3H1/SiALDH3H2, SiALDH2B1/SiALDH2B2 and SiALDH18B1/SiALDH18B2) are located in four pairs of segmental duplicated genome regions.

37.q: eight genes (SiALDH3E1/SiALDH3E2, SiALDH3H1/SiALDH3H2, SiALDH2B1/SiALDH2B2 and SiALDH18B1/SiALDH18B2) are located in four pairs of segmental duplicated genome regions.

38.q: SiALDH2C2 and SiALDH2C3 genes are identified as a tandem duplication in foxtail millet ALDH family.

39.q: low K elicited SiHAK1-meidated high-affinity K+ uptake activity in Cy162 yeast cells and Arabidopsis athak5 mutants.

40.q: low K elicited SiHAK1-meidated high-affinity K+ uptake activity in Cy162 yeast cells and Arabidopsis athak5 mutants.

41.q: SiNCED1 plays a positive role in the drought tolerance and seed dormancy of foxtail millet by modulating ABA biosynthesis

42.q: the phosphorylation of sucrose nonfermenting kinase 2s (SnRK2s), the central kinases involved in osmotic stress
